title: Berry Bliss
description: A citrusy and tender Peruvian dish featuring Saskatoon berries that are reduced with a touch of sugar for an indulgent dessert option suitable for those following a sugar-free diet.

ingredients.name: Caster Sugar (optional, for a touch of sweetness)

instructions: Rinse the Saskatoon berries and remove any stems or leaves. If using sugar, mix it with water to dissolve completely.
Pour the Saskatoon berries into a saucepan, then add the lemon juice, lime juice, and optional sugar mixture.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to low once it starts boiling.
Cook for about 10-15 minutes or until the berries are tender but not mushy. They will be soft when pierced with a fork.
Turn off the heat and let cool slightly before serving. The sauce can also be served hot, garnished with fresh berries if desired.
Serve immediately while warm for best texture and flavor.
